sources_exist.note: Four distinct source pages resolve, but the JFS Partners owner/succession URL returns an internal error. The Kaseya URL resolves only to a download landing page rather than exposing the report figures cited by the run.

figures_match_sources.note: CT Acquisitions supports 169 MSP deals in 2025, 69% PE involvement, and seven named active platforms; Aventis publishes implied MSP multiples by transaction deal size; Pharallax publishes small-MSP recurring-revenue benchmarks. The sources do not establish 15 active consolidators, the selected EBITDA-scoped buy/exit multiples, 35% material firm adoption, the historical RMM/PSA trajectory, 40% owners age 60+, a documented succession shortage, or the full pricing mix and trend figures.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The three-year t50 and bounded role-level fractions are directionally plausible, but the empirical base is not adequate for the score-affecting judgments. Current adoption and historical analogs rely on an inaccessible report body; 15 consolidators is extrapolated from seven named platforms; and founder age or sale intent is treated as proof of an internal-successor shortage. The terminal-value judgment also asserts regulatory accountability and durable channel positioning without direct cited support.

rubric_consistent.note: Deterministic dataset inputs match exactly, but B receives the documented-succession-shortage uplift even though the cited record does not document a shortage of internal successors. M applies deal-value buckets as EBITDA buckets and applies MSP recurring-revenue benchmarks to the broader blended 541512 target pool without adequate segmentation. ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL on A, B, S, and V is not disqualifying by itself, but it does not cure these input and rubric defects.

cross_checks_honest.note: All four mandatory cross-check fields are present, but they repeat unsupported claims as established evidence. no_proxy_confirmed relies on exact Kaseya figures not visible on the cited landing page, pricing_model_deflation repeats unsupported billing shares, and terminal_value treats M&A activity as evidence of durable end demand while also introducing uncited legal-accountability and platform-channel assertions.

source_audits.note: CT Acquisitions reports 169 MSP transactions in 2025, PE involvement in 69%, and seven verified active platforms: Evergreen, New Charter, Ntiva, Coretelligent, Thrive, Integris, and Magna5. It does not identify or substantiate the selected count of 15 qualifying active consolidators.

Aventis explicitly presents implied MSP multiples by transaction deal size: 5.2x below $5M and 6.8x for $5M-$20M. Those are not $1M-$10M EBITDA buckets, and the page does not establish a 5.5x median for the run's blended target tier.

The accessible Kaseya page is a report landing/download page stating that nearly 1,000 MSPs were surveyed and listing AI and automation as a report topic. It does not expose the quoted 30% tedious-task figure, the 25% material-change figure, or evidence for the selected 35% firm-level material-adoption input.

The 11.2x figure is an implied MSP benchmark for transactions above $500M in deal value, derived from public MSP references and size discounts. It is not evidence for an 11.0x exit multiple at $10M-plus EBITDA, and it does not segment the broader NAICS 541512 pool by recurring-revenue quality.

The accessible landing page contains no 2008-2016 RMM adoption series, no below-20%-to-above-80% trajectory, and no parallel PSA adoption timeline. A report title and topic list do not support the historical analog used to select t50.

The exact cited URL returns an internal error and cannot be audited. In any event, the run's quoted founder-age, firm-age, and five-year sale-intent observations would not by themselves measure a 40% age-60-plus share or document a shortage of internal successors.

Pharallax gives 65%-78% MRR as a target, 78%-90% as best-in-class, and 25%-40% project/break-fix revenue for small 5-to-10-person, $600K-$2M MSPs. It does not support the run's 82% total-income figure, project-margin decline from 23% to 12.9%, per-seat decline from 21% to 15%, or a 65%-82% MRR share for the required EBITDA target tier.

reasons: At inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://www.jfs-partners.com/post/msp-m-a-2026-why-the-window-is-still-wide-open-and-how-long-it-will-last), the exact page is inaccessible, so inputs.owners_60plus_pct.value=0.40 cannot be audited. Replace it with accessible MSP-owner age data or a transparent cohort estimate tied to the target population.

At inputs.succession_shortage_documented.value, the run assigns true based on founder age, firm age, and sale intent, none of which demonstrates that firms lack internal successors. Supply direct, accessible evidence of an internal-successor shortage or set the field false and remove the B uplift.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/private-equity-msp-2026/), the source names seven qualifying active platforms rather than 15. Identify the additional qualifying platforms with dated add-on evidence under the stated activity rule or use the documented count.

At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://www.kaseya.com/resource/2025-msp-benchmark-report/), the accessible page exposes no exact AI-adoption results and cannot support the 35% firm-level material-adoption estimate. Cite an accessible report table or primary survey measuring the rubric's material-use threshold.

At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://www.kaseya.com/resource/2025-msp-benchmark-report/), the accessible page contains none of the asserted RMM or PSA adoption trajectory. Replace it with a dated adoption series and an explicit calculation of the comparable 50%-adoption milestone.

At inputs.buy_mult.url (https://aventis-advisors.com/msp-valuation-multiples/), the run treats transaction deal-value buckets as $1M-$10M EBITDA buckets. Re-run with transaction multiples segmented by target EBITDA and recurring-revenue quality for the covered 541512 business mix.

At inputs.exit_mult.url (https://aventis-advisors.com/msp-valuation-multiples/), the cited 11.2x benchmark belongs to deals above $500M in transaction value, not platforms above $10M EBITDA. Use correctly scoped platform-exit comps and distinguish MRR-heavy MSPs from project, resale, and other systems-design revenue.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://pharallax.ai/guides/msp-revenue-benchmarks-2026/), the source is scoped to small MSPs and does not support several quoted trend figures or the selected 65%-82% MRR share for the target tier. Source the recurring, project, resale, and break-fix shares for relevant EBITDA-sized firms and label any remaining conversion as an estimate.

At inputs.terminal_value and cross_checks.terminal_value_justification, the run asserts that cyber-insurance, CMMC, and HIPAA require an accountable MSP and that vendors will preserve the channel, while also using acquisition activity as demand evidence. Cite direct regulatory/customer-accountability and platform-channel evidence; do not use M&A activity as a proxy for durable end demand.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, S, V
